items.q: What is the difference between CAD and CAM?
items.correct: CAD creates digital designs; CAM uses those designs to control manufacturing machines.
items.misconception: They are the same thing with different names.
items.random_wrong: CAD is for cars, CAM is for cameras.
items.why: Students treat the terms as synonymous because they often appear together. CAD = drawing/modelling software; CAM = machine control (CNC routers, 3D printers) - typically the second stage.

items.q: What does 'Rethink' mean in the 6 Rs?
items.correct: Questioning whether a product is necessary or could be designed differently for sustainability.
items.misconception: Trying a design again if it does not work first time.
items.why: Students apply 'rethink' to design iteration. In sustainability, it means challenging the NEED for a product altogether, or fundamentally rethinking how it's made - a more radical step than reducing materials.

items.q: Why can thermoplastics be recycled but thermosets cannot?
items.correct: Thermoplastics soften when heated and can be remoulded; thermosets have cross-links that lock the shape permanently.
items.misconception: Thermosets are too dangerous to recycle.
items.random_wrong: Thermoplastics are cheaper, so it is worth recycling them.
items.why: Students miss the chemistry. Thermosets undergo a one-way curing reaction creating cross-links; reheating them just degrades them without remelting. Thermoplastics have no cross-links so they remelt freely.

items.q: Which prototype tests how a product functions rather than how it looks?
items.why: It's Working prototype. “Appearance prototype” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which digital process is most commonly used for rapid prototyping at A-Level?
items.why: It's 3D printing. “Sand casting” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Approximately how many iterative cycles might a school design project undergo at A-Level?
items.why: The real figure is 3 cycles. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
